name: powerlifted-htg-grounder-work
gate: relaxed: every baseline-solved pair still solves with plan cost <= baseline; trajectory not gated
note: deterministic grounder_atoms is a SCREEN, not a gate: 'cheaper-per-fact' wins leave it flat while cutting time. Base = master 6a46eb8 (incl. merged search-time SBO/grounder-overhead opts).

description: baseline: master 6a46eb8 (incl. merged search-time SBO/grounder-overhead opts) + grounder meter; 5 sweeps via REPS=5 ./autoresearch.sh in a quiet window (loadavg ~1.1), gate passed all 5. NOTE ~28s not ~51s: the merged opts nearly halve wall-clock on this suite vs the stale base, while grounder_atoms is unchanged (47,644,479 / pushes 22,398,404 identical every sweep, 53% wasted productions -> redundant-work lever intact).

Exp1 (cheaper, counter FLAT): build join/project achiever bodies directly in the inline small_vector<int,2> via new Achievers(int,int,int,int)/Achievers(int,int,int) ctors, removing the per-produced-fact heap-allocated temporary std::vector<int> (join 2-int + project 1-int). grounder_atoms identical (47,644,479), so behavior-preserving. A/B was load-confounded on first try: cand window had rising load 0.59->1.41 -> DISCARD (cand 31.165 vs HEAD 30.728). Re-measured with a HEAD sandwich (HEAD before 30.728 / after 31.098, both quiet windows) around cand 29.622 -> KEEP. Pooled HEAD(10) median 31.012 vs cand(5) 29.622 = +4.48%, conf 2.20x, noise floor 2.47%. checks 62/62.

Exp2 (cheaper, counter FLAT): ReachedFacts::get_costs() returned std::vector<int> by value; changed to const-ref so product()'s min-cost scan stops copying the whole costs vector per condition per call. Removes a real per-product-call heap copy but only on the narrow product path (genome). A/B vs HEAD 79ee9c0: cand median 29.375 vs 29.506, +0.44% -> DISCARD (conf 0.17x, noise floor 4.2%). Sound but too small to clear suite noise. Reverted.

Exp3 (cheaper, counter FLAT): rewrote is_cheapest_path_to_achieve_fact to use phmap lazy_emplace, fusing the find()+insert() double hash/probe into one find_or_prepare_insert per NEW fact (~22.4M), and replacing the strictly-cheaper branch's erase+insert with an in-place const_cast set_cost (cost isn't hashed/compared; reached_facts achievers are never read back -- backchain reads achievers from Datalog::facts). Behavior-preserving: plan costs identical on heavy pairs d-13-2(79) and d-7-8(31), grounder_atoms flat. But the saved find-probe on small cache-hot genome args is too cheap: order-balanced interleaved full-suite A/B (9 rounds, alternating which binary runs first to cancel drift+position bias) gave paired C-H median -0.22s (~0.7%), mean -0.15s, with per-round noise +/-1-2s -> indistinguishable from zero. Naive 5v5 and per-pair screens were inconclusive/noisy. DISCARD. Reverted. NOTE methodology: always-H-first interleaving biased the 2nd-position sweep (thermal/cache); order-balancing is required on this box.

Exp4 (cheaper attempt -> REGRESSION): make reached_facts (flat_hash_set<Fact>) and newfacts reused members clear()'d per ground() call instead of local per-call allocation, to avoid re-malloc + growth-rehash every state eval (same pattern as JoinHashTable::clear). Counter flat, plan cost 79 preserved, peak_mem 65->60. But SLOWER: order-balanced interleaved A/B (7 rounds, quiet window, noise floor 0.44%) gave C-H = +0.32s (1.16% slower) EVERY round, conf 3.24x. Reason: clear() is O(capacity); carrying a bucket array sized to the largest eval penalizes the many small evals (cold/sparse table) more than a fresh right-sized set. DISCARD. Reverted.

Exp5 (cheaper attempt -> REGRESSION): in join(), hoist the partner-side (head_pos, partner_arg_pos) mapping out of the per-partner loop (precompute once per call in a reused buffer) instead of re-calling get_head_position_of_arg + is_object for every matched partner. Counter flat, plan cost 79 preserved. But SLOWER: order-balanced A/B (7 rounds, noise floor 0.32%) C-H = +0.19s (0.68% slower) EVERY round, conf 2.02x. Reason: position_of is a cache-hot <=4-elem scan (already cheap), and the precompute pass now runs UNCONDITIONALLY including for the many zero-partner join calls where the original did zero inner work. DISCARD. Reverted.

Exp6 (cheaper, counter FLAT) KEEP +3.69%: product()'s nullary/ground-head path recomputed the cheapest reached tuple per condition by re-scanning ALL reached tuples on EVERY product() call (O(n^2) over the grounding) AND get_costs() returned the whole cost vector by value (a heap copy per scan). Now ReachedFacts tracks (min_cost, min_fact_index) incrementally in push_back (strict '<' preserves the old first-wins tie-break), so the ground-head branch is O(#conditions); also split the all-conditions-nonempty check into its own pass and skip the min work + nullary_head_achievers alloc entirely for non-ground heads (min-scan is unused there). Behavior-preserving: grounder_atoms flat (47,644,479), plan costs preserved d-13-2 gbfs-ff=79 / d-7-8 gbfs-add=31, checks 62/62. Measured via order-balanced interleaved A/B (7 rounds, quiet window): C-H = -1.0s EVERY round, median 26.308 vs 27.315, conf 19.98x, noise floor 0.75%. Profile motivation: perf annotate showed product's hottest lines were the min-scan compare (weighted_grounder.cc:285-286) + get_costs copy. Committed 15b6379.

Exp7 (cheaper, counter FLAT) KEEP +4.39%: JoinHashTable::get_entries() looked up join partners with operator[], which INSERTS a spurious empty JoinHashEntry on a miss -- copying the vector<int> join key into the map and possibly rehashing. Zero-partner lookups are very common (a popped fact whose key has no partner yet), so this polluted the map on the hottest path. Switched the lookup to find() returning a static empty entry on miss (absent key yields the same empty range; insert side still uses operator[] correctly). Behavior-preserving: grounder_atoms flat (47,644,479), plan costs d-13-2 ff=79 / d-7-8 add=31, checks 62/62, peak_mem 65->61. Profile motivation: join was #1 at 16.84% self-time, all hot lines inside phmap SIMD probing (insert_fact_in_hash + get_facts_matching_key). Order-balanced interleaved A/B (7 rounds): C-H = -1.16s EVERY round, median 25.250 vs 26.408, conf 14.46x, noise floor 0.70%.
